assertions.description: The analyzer correctly identifies that SDA and SCL are single-pin global labels (connecting to IC1 TCA8418 only, with no pull-up resistors in this schematic sheet). The I2C pull-up resistors (R1 4.7k and R2 10k) are connected to INT and RESET nets respectively, not to SDA/SCL. The observation that SDA/SCL have no pull-ups is accurate for this leaf schematic.

INCORRECT: The lib_id 'kb5-rescue:SW_Push_45deg-sw_push_45deg-ESP32-S2-WROOM-rescue' contains the substring 'WROOM' (or similar ESP32 module name) in a rescue library name, causing the rf_chains detector to classify all 78-85 keyboard switches as RF transceivers. Affects all four keyboard schematics: ti92revive.kicad_sch (78 transceivers), keyboard.kicad_sch (74), ti92-reviceStable.sch (85), kb5.sch (63). These are all SW_Push_45deg push-button components connected in a keyboard matrix, not wireless transceivers. The rf_chains list should be empty for all of these files.

INCORRECT: The source schematic contains 88 non-power components (85 SW_Push_45deg switches + IC1 TCA8418 + R1 + R2) but the analyzer reports total_components=80 (77 'other' + 1 'ic' + 2 'resistor'). Eight keyboard switch components are silently dropped: kbALT1, kbD1, kbMode1, kbN2, kbRBrack1, kbX1, kf2, kwE1. Seven of these share x-coordinate 82.042mm in the schematic layout. The same pattern occurs in keyboard.kicad_sch (76 reported vs 85 actual, 9 missing). These missing references appear in no output lists (not in components, bom, missing_mpn, or annotation_issues).

INCORRECT: Component kbLBrack1 has value='(' in the source schematic (the open-parenthesis key on the TI-92 keyboard). The schematic analyzer misparses this as the start of an s-expression, yielding value="[['id', '1'], ['at', '62.738', '116.205', '0']]" in the output instead of the string '('. This affects both the components list and BOM entry. The same bug occurs in keyboard.kicad_sch where kbLBrack1 appears.

INCORRECT: Both ti92revive.kicad_sch and keyboard.kicad_sch have file_version=20200828, which is an early KiCad 6 development/nightly build format from mid-2020. The analyzer reports kicad_version='unknown' instead of identifying this as a KiCad 6 pre-release format. KiCad 6.0 was released in December 2021; version dates prior to that are development builds. The schema is identical to KiCad 6 and should be reported as '6 (pre-release)' or similar rather than 'unknown'.

All four keyboard schematics contain a TCA8418 8x10 keyboard controller IC (IC1) wired to row0-row7 and col0-col9 nets with 74-85 keyboard switches forming a full key matrix. The key_matrices detector returns empty ([]) for all files. The TCA8418 with its named row/col nets and associated switches is a textbook key matrix and should be detected. The design uses I2C (SDA/SCL) for host communication.
